business_problem: Bayer's agronomic advisors and frontline teams were spending hours or days navigating crop-protection labels that can exceed 100 pages, escalating to a small tech team and risking delays and errors when giving precise, label-accurate guidance across products, crops, regions, and regulations.
solution_summary: Bayer developed E.L.Y. Crop Protection (Mini), a domain-specialized small language model built on Microsoft Phi and hosted in Azure AI Foundry. The system ingests proprietary agronomic product label data and regulatory rules, fine-tuned to deliver precise crop protection guidance without hallucination. Frontline agricultural advisors query the model through E.L.Y. Copilot, which retrieves label-accurate answers in real time. Azure AI Foundry provides infrastructure for model versioning, compliance logging, and source-linked traceability. The solution maintains full IP ownership, audit trails, and rapid update cycles to incorporate label changes and regulatory shifts without retraining. Strategic retail partners access the model through secure APIs and access controls, enabling scaled deployment across the United States with measurable productivity gains.
business_outcome: Bayer is seeing 5–10% productivity gains among early users, resolving complex crop-protection questions in under 30 seconds instead of days or weeks, and is planning to expand the model to more retail partners while extending coverage to seeds, biologicals, carbon products, and additional languages.
metrics: Complex questions resolved in under 30 seconds vs days or weeks
architecture_clues: Small language model on Microsoft Phi architecture
Fine-tuned on proprietary Bayer label data and regulatory rules
Hosted in Azure AI Foundry Models with versioning and IP control
Surfaced via existing E.L.Y. Copilot interface and exposed to partners through secure APIs
Aligned to Microsoft's solution accelerator for regulated AI deployments
evidence_quotes: We estimate 5-10% productivity gains among early users. Complex questions that once took days or weeks to resolve now take under 30 seconds.

We fine-tuned Microsoft Phi using our internal datasets. We worked closely with Microsoft’s Cloud for Industry team to shape the model’s architecture and ensure it aligned with our compliance and operational needs.
Azure AI Foundry also gives us IP ownership and lets us control updates, access, and traceability.
The model is hosted in Azure AI Foundry Models, allowing Bayer to retain full IP control while enabling rapid iteration, versioning, and traceability.

solution_components.name: Microsoft Phi
solution_components.role: Small language model foundation fine-tuned exclusively on proprietary agronomic product label data and regulatory rules.

solution_components.name: Azure AI Foundry
solution_components.role: Hosts the domain-specialized model with version control, compliance logging, and IP ownership for rapid iteration and updates.

solution_components.name: Azure AI Foundry Models
solution_components.role: Provides the catalog and publishing infrastructure for sharing the fine-tuned model with strategic agricultural retailer partners.

solution_components.name: Microsoft Cloud for Industry
solution_components.role: Provides compliance architecture guidance and managed services to ensure the solution meets regulated agricultural use case requirements.

data_flow: Proprietary product label data and regulatory rules are fine-tuned into the Microsoft Phi model within Azure AI Foundry. When advisors query E.L.Y. Copilot with crop protection questions, the model retrieves contextual label information, processes through the fine-tuned Phi architecture, and returns compliant, label-accurate answers sourced to specific documents. All responses are audit-logged and version-controlled in Azure AI Foundry, enabling rapid updates when label changes or regulatory shifts occur without retraining from scratch.
integration_points: Proprietary product label databases and regulatory compliance frameworks
E.L.Y. Copilot interface for internal and external advisors
Agricultural retailer partner platforms and reseller channels
Field advisory tools and existing agronomic systems
